title: Profile README
content:
### Special Repository

Create a repository named exactly `username/username` (e.g., `octocat/octocat`) with a `README.md`. GitHub displays this README on your profile page above the repository grid.

### Profile README Markdown

Showcase your skills, projects, and activity. Include:
- Bio and current role
- Skills and technologies
- Pinned repository links
- GitHub stats and contribution graphs

### GitHub Stats Widgets

Use external services to display dynamic badges:

```markdown
![GitHub stats](https://github-readme-stats.vercel.app/api?username=octocat&show_icons=true)

![Top Langs](https://github-readme-stats.vercel.app/api/top-langs/?username=octocat)
```

Popular widgets: [github-readme-stats](https://github.com/anuraghazra/github-readme-stats), [gh-profile-badge](https://github.com/schneefux/gh-profile-badge).

### Visitor Badges

```markdown
![Profile views](https://komarev.com/ghpvc/?username=octocat)
```

### GitHub Activity Graph

```markdown
![Activity graph](https://github-readme-activity-graph.vercel.app/graph?username=octocat)
```

quiz.question: What name must a special repository have for a profile README?
Which external service provides the popular GitHub Stats card?
